body:
## Goal
Provide a dependency-light Node.js JSON REST API where users sign up, sign in, and manage their own private notes, with all data surviving a server restart.

## Spec
ESM Node.js HTTP server using only the standard library (node:http, node:crypto) plus the built-in node:sqlite module for an on-disk SQLite database file (no external web framework, no in-memory store). Listens on process.env.PORT (default 3000).

Endpoints (all request/response bodies JSON):
- POST /signup {username, password} → 201 {id, username}. Reject missing/blank fields with 400; reject an existing username with 409.
- POST /login {username, password} → 200 {session} where session is an opaque token (crypto.randomBytes hex). Wrong credentials → 401. Malformed body → 400.
- GET /me → 200 {id, username} for the bearer-authenticated user.
- POST /notes {title, body} → 201 with the created note {id, title, body} owned by the caller. Missing fields → 400.
- GET /notes → 200 JSON array of the caller's own notes only (never another user's).

Auth invariants: sessions are presented as `Authorization: Bearer <token>`. /me and both /notes endpoints require a valid session; a missing/unknown token → 401. A user may only ever read/write their own notes (ownership enforced by session→user_id). Passwords are never stored in plaintext — hash with a per-user salt via node:crypto (scrypt). Unknown route/method → 404/405; body that is not valid JSON → 400. Sessions persist in the SQLite file so they survive restart.

## Changes
- `src/server.js` — creates
- `src/db.js` — creates
- `src/router.js` — creates
- `package.json` — refactors-existing
- `test/api.test.js` — creates

## Acceptance
- [ ] AC-1: Server starts with `npm start` and listens on process.env.PORT, defaulting to 3000 when PORT is unset.
- [ ] AC-2: POST /signup with {username, password} returns 201 and JSON {id, username}; a duplicate username returns 409 and a missing/blank username or password returns 400.
- [ ] AC-3: POST /login with correct credentials returns 200 and JSON {session}; wrong credentials return 401.
- [ ] AC-4: GET /me with a valid `Authorization: Bearer <session>` returns 200 and {id, username}; a missing or invalid token returns 401.
- [ ] AC-5: POST /notes with a valid session and {title, body} returns 201 and the created note including its id; missing fields return 400.
- [ ] AC-6: GET /notes with a valid session returns 200 and a JSON array containing only the caller's own notes, never another user's.
- [ ] AC-7: Users, notes, and sessions persist to an on-disk store and remain readable after the server process is stopped and restarted.
- [ ] AC-8: Passwords are not stored in plaintext in the on-disk store.
- [ ] AC-9: Requests with malformed JSON bodies or unknown routes are rejected with a 4xx status rather than crashing the server.

## Verify
- npm start (e2e)
- node --test test/api.test.js (contract)
- npm run typecheck (validate)

## Non-Goals
- No external web framework or npm runtime dependencies (standard library only)
- No password reset, email verification, or session expiry/rotation
- No note editing or deletion endpoints (create + list only)
- No pagination, search, or rate limiting

<!-- meta: {"reason_to_exist":"A minimal, self-contained multi-user notes API that persists across restarts without pulling in any web framework."} -->

<!-- plan-story: 69bb999f1a415903 -->

title: JSON REST API for user accounts and personal notes with on-disk persistence
